skill.name: css-colors
skill.description: Instructions on how to add new colors for CSS rules
skill.summary: Instructions on how to add new colors for CSS rules
skill.author: Crossbill-Highlights

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at SKILL.md lines 3 and 7. Both are false positives caused by CSS color guidance, with no cryptographic APIs, executable code, or prompt injection found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 3 only describes adding colors for CSS rules. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain metadata about CSS color guidance. I found no code or cryptographic API on this line.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 7 advises using theme colors instead of direct color codes. This is a design-system rule, not cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text references CSS color codes in component code. I found no execution, data access, network use, or cryptographic behavior.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need consistent color usage across CSS and components. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use theme colors instead of hardcoded values.
actual_capabilities: Guides assistants to prefer Material UI theme colors when adding CSS rules.
Directs color changes toward the project theme instead of component-level literals.
Explains when to add new values to the customColors section.
Supports consistent color naming across frontend style updates.
Helps review color changes for design-system alignment.
limitations: Does not define the actual color palette or token names.
Does not modify files without a separate user request.
Does not validate visual contrast or accessibility by itself.
Depends on the existing theme structure in the target project.

use_cases.title: Add a New UI Color
use_cases.description: Add a color while keeping component styles aligned with the shared theme.

use_cases.title: Review Styling Consistency
use_cases.description: Check whether color changes use theme values instead of direct color codes.

use_cases.title: Maintain Design Tokens
use_cases.description: Decide when a new color belongs in customColors for reuse.

prompt_templates.title: Use Theme Color
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple color change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this component color using the project theme. Avoid direct color codes in component code.

prompt_templates.title: Add Reusable Color
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request when an existing theme value is missing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a reusable color for this UI state. Use the theme customColors section if no existing theme color fits.

prompt_templates.title: Review Color Usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a proposed styling change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these style changes for hardcoded colors. Suggest theme-based replacements where possible.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Color Token Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a migration from direct component color values to theme colors. Group changes by existing theme values and needed customColors entries.

output_examples.input: Add a warning color to a button state.
output_examples.output: The assistant should first look for a suitable theme warning color. If none exists, it should add a reusable customColors entry.

output_examples.input: Review a component that uses direct color values.
output: Recommends adding a named custom color only when reuse is expected.

best_practices: Check existing Material UI theme colors before adding a new value.
Use named theme colors in component styles instead of direct values.
Add new shared colors to customColors when they need reuse.
anti_patterns: Hardcoding direct color values inside component code.
Creating one-off colors when an existing theme value works.
Adding theme colors without clear naming or reuse intent.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps assistants keep CSS color changes aligned with the project theme.

faq.question: Does it create new theme colors automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides the assistant to add them only when an existing theme color is not suitable.

faq.question: Can it replace all hardcoded colors?
faq.answer: It can guide replacement work, but the assistant still needs project context and user approval.

faq.question: Does it check accessibility contrast?
faq.answer: No. Ask for contrast checks separately if accessibility validation is required.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains only CSS color guidance and no executable behavior.
